INJECTION MOLDING KNOWLEDGE BASE
Industrial AI Root Cause Analysis System

--------------------------------------------------
PROCESS OVERVIEW
--------------------------------------------------

Injection molding is a manufacturing process used to produce parts by injecting molten material into a mold.

The process generally includes the following stages:

1. Plasticizing
Plastic pellets are melted in the machine barrel using heat and screw rotation.

2. Injection
The molten plastic is injected into the mold cavity under high pressure.

3. Packing / Holding
Pressure is maintained to compensate for material shrinkage.

4. Cooling
The part cools and solidifies inside the mold.

5. Ejection
The finished part is ejected from the mold.

--------------------------------------------------
IMPORTANT MACHINE PARAMETERS
--------------------------------------------------

Cylinder Temperature
Controls the temperature of the molten plastic.

Too High:
- Material degradation
- Burn marks

Too Low:
- Poor flow
- Short shots

--------------------------------------------------

Mold Temperature
Controls cooling speed.

Too High:
- Longer cycle time
- Warpage risk

Too Low:
- Surface defects
- Poor part quality

--------------------------------------------------

Injection Speed
Determines how fast material enters the mold.

Too Fast:
- Flash
- Burn marks

Too Slow:
- Incomplete filling
- Flow marks

--------------------------------------------------

Holding Pressure
Maintains pressure during cooling.

Too High:
- Flash
- Internal stress

Too Low:
- Sink marks
- Voids

--------------------------------------------------

Cooling Time
Determines how long the part remains in the mold.

Too Short:
- Warping
- Deformation

Too Long:
- Reduced productivity

--------------------------------------------------
COMMON DEFECTS
--------------------------------------------------

Short Shot
The mold cavity is not completely filled.

Possible causes:
- Low injection pressure
- Low material temperature
- Slow injection speed

--------------------------------------------------

Flash
Excess material leaks outside the mold.

Possible causes:
- High injection pressure
- Mold misalignment
- High holding pressure

--------------------------------------------------

Sink Marks
Depressions on the part surface.

Possible causes:
- Insufficient packing pressure
- Thick sections cooling unevenly

--------------------------------------------------

Warping
Part deformation after cooling.

Possible causes:
- Uneven cooling
- Mold temperature imbalance

--------------------------------------------------

Burn Marks
Dark discoloration on the part.

Possible causes:
- Trapped air
- Excessive temperature
- High injection speed

--------------------------------------------------
ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS PRINCIPLES
--------------------------------------------------

Industrial root cause analysis involves identifying which machine parameters are responsible for defects.

AI models analyze historical production data and detect relationships between machine settings and product quality.

Typical analysis methods include:

- Feature importance analysis
- Correlation analysis
- Machine learning models
- SHAP explainability analysis

--------------------------------------------------

Goal of this AI system:

Predict product quality and identify the machine parameters responsible for defects.
